functions.name: UserAdd
functions.reason: creates a new acluser via calloc and returns it through the **up output parameter

cfr.name: UserAdd
cfr.documentation: be "none", as this represents the NULL-pointer when dealing with groups. He has default rights, determined by umask. Returns: 0 - on success 1 - he is already there -1 - he still does not exist (didn't get memory) =====================================================================

functions.name: UserDel
functions.reason: doc says it destroys all the user's permissions and detaches him; ends lifetime of the whole user object

cfr.name: UserDel
cfr.documentation: UserDel- Remove a user from the list. Destroy all his permissions and completely detach him from the session. Returns 0 - success -1 - he who does not exist cannot be removed =====================================================================

functions.name: UserFreeCopyBuffer
functions.reason: frees the user's copy buffer field and clears references; field-level release

cfr.name: UserFreeCopyBuffer
cfr.documentation: UserFreeCopyBuffer- frees user buffer Also removes any references into the users copybuffer Returns: 0 - if the copy buffer was really deleted. -1 - cannot remove something that does not exist =====================================================================

functions.name: FreeWindowAcl
functions.reason: frees the window's ACL bitfield members

functions.name: CatExtra

functions.name: ReadFile
functions.reason: Documented to return an allocated buffer holding file contents

cfr.name: ReadFile
cfr.documentation: returns an allocated buffer which holds a copy of the file named filename. lenp (if nonzero) points to a location, where the buffer size should be stored.

functions.name: LayerCleanupMemory
functions.reason: Frees memory held by the passed layer's fields

functions.name: FreeLayoutCv
functions.reason: Recursively frees canvas memory of the passed canvas

functions.name: CreateLayout
functions.reason: Allocates and returns a new Layout object

functions.name: RemoveLayout
functions.reason: Ends lifetime of a layout, freeing it and its nested canvases

functions.name: glist_add_row
functions.reason: Allocates and returns a new ListRow inserted into the list

functions.name: glist_remove_rows
functions.reason: Frees all rows held in the list data

functions.name: gl_Window_remove
functions.reason: Removes and frees a window row from the list

functions.name: gl_Window_free
functions.reason: Frees the list data's allocated content

functions.name: logfopen
functions.reason: calloc-allocates a Log object and returns it to the caller

functions.name: logfclose
functions.reason: closes the file resource and frees the whole Log object, ending its lifetime

functions.name: FreePaster
functions.reason: releases the paster's buffer and dequeues its event; frees caller-owned fields

functions.name: SaveStr
functions.reason: strdup wrapper returning newly allocated string

functions.name: SaveStrn
functions.reason: strndup wrapper returning newly allocated string

functions.name: FindKtab
functions.reason: allocates a new action table via malloc/SaveStr and returns it when create is set

functions.name: ClearAction
functions.reason: frees the action's owned fields

functions.reason: Allocates new arg array via malloc/SaveStr and returns it to caller.

functions.name: InputSu
functions.reason: Allocates acluser via calloc and returns it through output parameter up.

functions.name: FreeMline
functions.reason: Releases the memory fields of a caller-owned mline.

functions.name: xrealloc
functions.reason: Reallocates memory and returns the (re)allocated pointer to caller.

functions.name: FreeAltScreen
functions.reason: Frees the alternate-screen buffers held by a window, releasing nested fields not the window itself.

functions.name: Free
functions.reason: Macro that frees the pointer and nulls it; a deliberate free wrapper.

cfr.name: Free
cfr.documentation: here comes my own Free: jw.

functions.name: CreateTransTable
functions.reason: Parses string and builds a translation table with calloc/malloc that persists; creates owned memory (FreeTransTable releases it). Returns int status, so allocator-leaning over non_memory.

functions.name: realloc
functions.reason: Reallocation wrapper returning a pointer to (re)allocated memory transferred to caller.

functions.name: MakeWindow
functions.reason: Allocates a Window object (calloc), initializes it, adds to list and returns its identifier; creates an owned object.

cfr.name: MakeWindow
cfr.documentation: Umask & wlock are set for the user of the display, The display d (if specified) switches to that window.

functions.name: FreeWindow
functions.reason: Ends lifetime of whole Window object, releasing many nested resources (paster, pseudowin, acl, layers); interface is object destruction.

functions.name: FreePseudowin
functions.reason: Releases the pseudo-window resources/fields of the passed window object.

functions.name: wmb_create
functions.reason: Allocates and initializes a new WinMsgBuf and returns it to caller; documented as freed by wmb_free.

cfr.name: wmb_create
cfr.documentation: Allocate and initialize to the empty string a new window message buffer. The return value must be freed using wmbc_free.

functions.name: wmb_expand
functions.reason: Grows the buffer's owned memory via realloc to hold at least min bytes; acquires/extends dynamic memory.

cfr.name: wmb_expand
cfr.documentation: Attempts to expand the buffer to hold at least MIN bytes. The new size of the buffer is returned, which may be unchanged from the original size if additional memory could not be allocated.

functions.name: wmb_free
functions.reason: Frees memory allocated to the given window buffer; a fairly direct free wrapper for the buffer object.

cfr.name: wmb_free
cfr.documentation: Deinitialize and free memory allocated to the given window buffer

functions.name: wmbc_create
functions.reason: Allocates and initializes a buffer context and returns it; documented as freed by wmbc_free.

cfr.name: wmbc_create
cfr.documentation: Allocate and initialize a buffer context for the given buffer. The return value must be freed using wmbc_free.

functions.name: wmbc_free
functions.reason: Deinitializes and frees a previously allocated context passed in by the caller.

cfr.name: wmbc_free
cfr.documentation: Deinitializes and frees previously allocated context. The contained buffer must be freed separately; this function will not do so for you.
